title: Pronouncing the Past
learningGoal: Master the three different sounds of the '-ed' ending (/t/, /d/, /id/) and the pronunciation of common irregular past tense verbs.
instructions: 過去形の「-ed」は、単語によって「トゥ」「ドゥ」「イド」という3つの異なる発音になります。また、wentやateのような不規則動詞の発音も練習します。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice the three sounds of '-ed' and the pronunciation of irregular verbs. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The '-ed' sounds like a sharp 't'. Watch-t.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'watch-ed' (ウォッチド) with an extra vowel.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 遊んだ、プレイした（play の過去形）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The '-ed' sounds like a soft 'd'. Play-d.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'play-do' with an 'o' at the end.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 欲しかった（want の過去形）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Because 'want' ends with a 't', the '-ed' adds a full syllable sounding like 'id'. Want-id.
Make sure the 't' at the end is crisp but quiet. W-en-t.
It is pronounced exactly the same as the number 8 (eight).
It is a long 'oh' sound, exactly like the tool you use to cut wood (saw). S-aw.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: しなかった（did not の短縮）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Did-int. The 'd' in the middle is very soft.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: だった（am / is の過去形）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Wuz. Make sure it sounds like a 'z' at the end, not an 's'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 持っていた（have の過去形）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 'a' is short, like in 'cat'. H-a-d.

expectedPronunciationText: watched played wanted went ate saw didn't was were had

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You clearly distinguished the different -ed sounds and the irregular verbs.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Pay close attention to 'watched' (sounds like 't') and 'played' (sounds like 'd'). Do not add an extra 'o' at the end.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember that 'ate' is pronounced exactly like the number 8.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: watched played wanted went ate saw didn't was were had
acceptedVariants: watched played wanted went eight saw didn't was were had
watched played wanted went ate saw did not was were had
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: watched played wanted went ate saw didn't was were had
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: wacht playd wantid went ate saw didint wuz wer had

audioAssets.audioTranscript: watched, played, wanted, went, ate, saw, didn't, was, were, had

hintText: If the base verb ends in a 't' or 'd' (like want or need), the -ed sounds like 'id' (want-id, need-id). Otherwise, it's just a 't' or 'd' sound.
